question.text: What is the minimum net worth requirement for a clearing member of the derivatives clearing corporation as per the L.C. Gupta Committee?

answer.text: Rs. 300 lakhs (Rs. 3 crore) minimum net worth for clearing members
Rs. 100 lakhs (Rs. 1 crore) minimum net worth for clearing members
Rs. 500 lakhs (Rs. 5 crore) minimum net worth for all trading and clearing members
No net worth requirement was specified by the L.C. Gupta Committee

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The L.C. Gupta Committee prescribed Rs. 300 lakhs (Rs. 3 crore) as the minimum net worth for clearing members of the derivatives clearing corporation. SEBI has not specified separate net worth for trading members (only clearing members need this). Self-clearing members need Rs. 1 crore. **The Trap:** Rs. 1 crore is for self-clearing members, not clearing members. SEBI does NOT have a separate net worth requirement for trading members (only exchanges may set their own). **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII Ch8 covers net worth requirements. The same SEBI framework applies to clearing members across all derivative segments. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Net worth is computed as per SEBI regulations — total assets minus total liabilities, with specific exclusions. CMs must furnish auditor's certificates every 6 months.
